description:
Retire `Store::load_config`'s XDG synthesizer (the temporary `Config` view built from `RepoJson` + `ProjectConfig` to keep legacy call sites compiling through Phase 1B). Replace each `store.load_config()?` with the right narrow `EffectiveConfig` read, so the binary stops translating between schemas at runtime.

## Scope

15+ call sites: `review_close.rs`, `review.rs`, `review_safety.rs`, `store.rs` (`worktrees_root`), `doctor.rs`, `commands/ready_cmd.rs`, `commands/sync.rs` (×3), `commands/basic.rs`, `commands/plumbing.rs`, `commands/lifecycle.rs`, `commands/half_push.rs`, `commands/claim.rs`.

For each: identify which fields the call site actually reads from the synthesized `Config` (most read just `worktree_dir`, `integration_branch`, `delivery_mode`, or one of the `require_remote_on_*` booleans) and rewrite to read from `store.load_effective_config()` returning `EffectiveConfig`.

Then drop `synthesize_xdg_config` from `store.rs` and inline `load_config` on the legacy path (or retire `load_config` entirely once no caller remains; the legacy path becomes `Config::load(store.config_path())` at the single `bl migrate` site).

## Why it can land alone

By the time Phase 1B-5 has flipped `cmd_init` to XDG, every running clone reads through the synthesizer. This slice is a refactor that strips the translation layer; behavior unchanged.

## Dependency

Depends on Phase 1B-5 (the flip). Before the flip, legacy call sites still need `Config`-shaped reads.

## Out of scope

- Any field added to `EffectiveConfig` — its schema is fixed by SPEC §6.5.
- `bl remaster` / `bl --bare` XDG — sibling Phase 1B-7.
title: Phase 1B-6: migrate load_config call sites to EffectiveConfig (retire the XDG synthesizer)
